summary: Iteration 2 (feature addition) fully validated. Ran regression (25/25 pytest pass) + 8 new iter2 backend tests (8/8 pass) + 1 ad-hoc auth rate-limit burst script (30x 401 then 429 with Retry-After) + Playwright UI test of /app/connect (all sections + create-bundle + download-bundle-{id} working). CLI modules (mcp_stdio.py, importer.py, benchmark/harness.py) all import cleanly. Object storage initializes at startup. All new features shipped as specified.

minor.issue: storage.put_object is synchronous requests.put() executed via asyncio.to_thread — OK for a serverless-scale API, but under heavy concurrent load it will block a thread-pool worker per upload. Consider aiohttp/httpx.AsyncClient for pure async.

In-memory sliding window is per-process; if backend ever scales to >1 replica behind a load balancer, effective per-IP limits will multiply by replica count. Fine for single-container preview; document limitation. Also, the _buckets defaultdict grows unbounded (key never GCed) — small leak over long-running processes. Consider periodic prune of empty deques.

Auth rate limit uses key `auth:{ip}` for BOTH /api/auth/register and /api/auth/login combined. That's fine, but means a user in a heavy CI/test loop can lock out real users behind the same NAT. Not a bug, but worth logging.

design_issues.screen: Connect page — Cloud export bundles list
issues: Bundle rows show only 'created_at · N facts · N events · KB' but no bundle id/preview. Downloading is fine but users cannot visually distinguish two same-size bundles from same second (rare). Consider showing short id prefix (b.id.slice(0,8)).

EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is shown as literal 'sk-emergent-...' placeholder in the snippet. If a user copies verbatim into claude_desktop_config.json, stdio server will fail. A one-line note above the snippet ('replace sk-emergent-... with your key') would help.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Show bundle id prefix in the Connect page bundles list for disambiguation.
OPTIONAL: Add inline note next to stdio EMERGENT_LLM_KEY placeholder telling the user to replace it.
OPTIONAL: Periodically prune ratelimit._buckets empty deques to avoid slow memory growth over weeks of uptime.
NO BLOCKING ISSUES. Iteration 2 features are production-ready.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py POST /api/export/bundle correctly wraps storage.put_object in asyncio.to_thread (good — sync requests lib doesn't block the event loop). Catches Exception and re-raises as 502 — good error boundary.
server.py list_export_bundles projects out _id via {'_id':0} and sorts by created_at DESC — matches spec ('newest first').
server.py download_export_bundle correctly filters by BOTH id AND vault_id, so a valid bundle id from another user returns 404. Verified via test_bundle_vault_isolation. Solid isolation.
ratelimit.py sliding-window uses time.time() + deque + popleft() — O(1) amortized. Retry-After header is int seconds — correct HTTP semantics.

ratelimit.client_ip trusts the first hop of X-Forwarded-For — fine behind a trusted ingress like Kubernetes, but do not deploy this behind an untrusted proxy without validation (users can spoof X-Forwarded-For and bypass the limit).

storage.py caches _storage_key in a module-global — good for reuse, but not thread-safe if init_storage is called concurrently on a cold start. asyncio.to_thread + a shared cache is acceptable because init only happens once at startup.
mcp_stdio.py properly reuses TOOLS and _dispatch_tool from mcp.py — no drift risk between HTTP and stdio transports. Vault resolution via MCP_TOKEN env is correct.
importer.py heuristic column classifier is reasonable but silent on ambiguous rows (skips tables without recognized columns and prints them). Good defensive behavior for an unknown schema.
server.py CORS still uses allow_origins from env with allow_credentials=False — consistent with iter1. JWT-in-header is fine.

server.py rate_limit_middleware runs BEFORE routes but the per-vault ratelimit.check(save:*, ctx:*) runs inside endpoints AFTER auth resolution. Correct ordering — IP burst is capped first, then per-vault limits.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all Connect page testids present + create/download/regen flows work)

test_credentials: Seeded admin admin@memoryvault.dev / admin123 works. Ad-hoc TEST_i2_* / TEST_a_* / TEST_b_* users created by fixtures.
seed_data_creation: Iter2 tests create 2 fresh users (TEST_i2_x_*, TEST_i2_y_*) and 2+ export bundles. Regression suite creates its own TEST_a_*, TEST_b_* users. UI test used seeded admin and created 1 bundle via UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter2 automated regression = /app/backend/tests/test_iter2_features.py (8 tests, 6.6s) + /app/backend/tests/test_memoryvault.py (25 tests, 15s). Burst auth rate-limit test at /app/backend/tests/burst_auth_ratelimit.py — run in isolation because it consumes the 30/60s auth IP window (sleeps 65s at start). Rate limits: auth 30/60s per IP, mcp 120/60s per IP, /memory/save 30/60s per vault, /context-pack 60/60s per vault. Object storage: emergent internal, path pattern memoryvault/exports/{vault_id}/{bundle_id}.json. Bundles are soft-deletable via is_deleted flag (not exposed as endpoint yet). Frontend token key: localStorage['mv_token'].
rca of the issue: No issues encountered. Rate limiter, export bundles (with vault isolation), and Connect page UI all working per spec on first run.
